proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_035 (binary, ~7.2% prevalence) is associated with a higher rate of objective_response than its absence.
feature_057 (3-level ordinal) is associated with objective_response, with higher levels having lower response rates (a monotonic gradient).
feature_011 (continuous, range 0-23.6) is negatively associated with objective_response: higher values correspond to lower response.
feature_006 (continuous, 20-100) is negatively associated with objective_response.
feature_005 (race; categorical white/black/asian/hispanic/other) shows differences in objective_response across categories.
feature_087 (insurance; medicaid/medicare/private/uninsured) shows differences in objective_response across categories.

analyses.code: z-test of proportions on objective_response by feature_035
analyses.result_summary: Response rate 22.1% with feature_035=1 vs 16.5% with feature_035=0 in 50000 patients (n=3624 with feature_035=1).

analyses.code: chi-square test on crosstab(feature_057, objective_response)
analyses.result_summary: Response rates: feature_057=0 -> 21.3%, =1 -> 15.5%, =2 -> 11.2% (clear monotonic gradient).

analyses.code: Welch t-test on feature_011 between responders and non-responders
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_011 = 3.38 in responders vs 3.95 in non-responders.

analyses.code: Welch t-test on feature_006 between responders and non-responders
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_006 = 58.2 in responders vs 60.3 in non-responders.

analyses.code: chi-square on crosstab(feature_005, objective_response)
analyses.result_summary: Response rates: white 17.3%, hispanic 16.7%, asian 16.3%, black 15.7%, other 14.7%.

analyses.code: chi-square on crosstab(feature_087, objective_response)
analyses.result_summary: Response rates: medicare 17.2%, medicaid 16.9%, private 16.7%, uninsured 16.6%; small differences.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutual adjustment in a multivariable logistic regression of objective_response on all features (one-hot for categoricals/multi-level ordinals), feature_057, feature_035, feature_011, feature_006, feature_099, feature_063, and feature_092 will remain independently significant predictors.
feature_099 (continuous, 1.7-5.5) is positively associated with objective_response after adjustment.
feature_063 (continuous, range 0-300, mean 6.1) is negatively associated with objective_response after adjustment.
feature_092 (continuous, 0.5-500, mean 20) is negatively associated with objective_response after adjustment.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(objective_response, X) where X includes all 124 features standardized/one-hot encoded (147 columns)
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logistic regression: feature_057_2 coef -0.769 (p=4e-77), feature_057_1 -0.385 (p=3e-51), feature_035 +0.360 (p=3e-17), feature_011 -0.157 per SD (p=7e-35), feature_006 -0.112 per SD (p=3e-20). 9 predictors remained significant at p<0.001.

analyses.code: coefficient for standardized feature_099 in adjusted logistic regression
analyses.result_summary: feature_099 coef +0.059 per SD (positive direction), independently significant.

analyses.code: coefficient for standardized feature_063 in adjusted logistic regression
analyses.result_summary: feature_063 coef -0.066 per SD; higher values lower the odds of response.

analyses.code: coefficient for standardized feature_092 in adjusted logistic regression
analyses.result_summary: feature_092 coef -0.058 per SD; higher values lower the odds of response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of feature_035 on objective_response is consistent across feature_057 strata (no qualitative interaction): feature_035=1 raises response in feature_057=0, feature_057=1, and feature_057=2 alike.
On the multiplicative (log-odds) scale, the interaction feature_035 x feature_057 is not statistically significant.
The 'treatment-like' binary feature_093 (~8% prevalence) is also independently positively associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: Stratified response rates by feature_057 within feature_035 levels
analyses.result_summary: feature_057=0: f035=1 26.97% vs f035=0 20.82% (diff +6.15pp). feature_057=1: 20.48% vs 15.14% (+5.35pp). feature_057=2: 15.31% vs 10.88% (+4.43pp). Direction consistent across strata.

Interaction terms f035 x f057_1 and f035 x f057_2 had coefficients +0.028 (p=0.75) and +0.053 (p=0.71); no significant multiplicative interaction. Treatment effect operates on the log-odds scale uniformly across stages.
feature_093 coef +0.125 (OR ~1.13) in fully adjusted model; 22.1% vs 18.4% univariate; +1.7pp absolute.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_035 is preserved across tertiles of feature_011 (continuous biomarker), with no significant feature_035 x feature_011 interaction.
There is no significant feature_035 x feature_006 interaction; the treatment effect of feature_035 is independent of feature_006.
feature_011 and feature_006 are largely independent prognostic biomarkers (their joint effects are approximately additive on the log-odds scale).

analyses.code: Tertile-stratified absolute differences and Logit(y ~ f035 + f011_z + f035:f011_z)
analyses.result_summary: Across tertiles of feature_011: low diff +6.12pp (24.41% vs 18.29%), mid +6.96pp (24.02% vs 17.06%), high +3.54pp (17.63% vs 14.09%). Interaction term coef -0.054 (p=0.225); absolute diffs attenuate at high f011 but multiplicative interaction not significant.
Interaction coef +0.036 (p=0.387); no evidence of effect modification.
Interaction coef +0.021 per SD-product (p=0.093); marginally suggestive but not significant; main effects unchanged.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for feature_057, feature_035, feature_011, and feature_006, the racial groups in feature_005 do NOT differ significantly in objective_response (no adjusted disparity).
After adjustment for prognostic factors and feature_078 (likely age), the insurance categories in feature_087 do NOT differ significantly in objective_response.
feature_078 (age, 18-92) shows essentially no main effect on objective_response.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ race_dummies + f057 + f011_z + f006_z + f035)
analyses.result_summary: All race coefficients non-significant: black -0.029 (p=0.64), hispanic +0.041 (p=0.49), other -0.116 (p=0.26), white +0.078 (p=0.13).

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ ins_dummies + f057 + f011_z + f006_z + f035 + f078_z)
analyses.result_summary: Insurance coefficients all near 0 and non-significant: medicare +0.022 (p=0.56), private -0.020 (p=0.59), uninsured -0.012 (p=0.86).

analyses.code: Quartile crosstab of feature_078 vs objective_response and standardized coef in adjusted model
analyses.result_summary: Response by feature_078 quartile: 16.7%, 17.4%, 16.6%, 16.9%; adjusted coef +0.004 per SD (p=0.75). No association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A consolidated multivariable model containing feature_057, feature_035, feature_093, feature_011, feature_006, feature_099, feature_063, feature_092, feature_014, feature_121, feature_125 fits substantially better than a null model and each of these terms remains an independent predictor.
feature_125 (5-level ordinal, 0-4) shows a small monotonic decreasing trend in objective_response across levels.
feature_014 (binary, 40% prevalence) is a small independent positive predictor of objective_response.
feature_121 (binary, 8% prevalence) is a small independent negative predictor of objective_response.

analyses.code: Logit with the listed predictors; pseudo-R^2 reported
analyses.result_summary: Full consolidated model: pseudo R^2 = 0.0187, log-likelihood -22292; all listed terms independently significant or near-significant.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ f125_linear); response rates by level: 17.4, 16.4, 16.7, 16.8, 15.7%
analyses.result_summary: Linear-in-level coef -0.023 per unit (p=0.038); small but statistically significant negative trend.

analyses.code: coef of feature_014 in consolidated model
coef of feature_121 in consolidated model

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among low-prevalence binary features (0.5-20% prevalence), no additional binary feature beyond feature_035 and feature_093 reaches strong (p<0.01) independent association with objective_response after adjustment for feature_057, feature_035, feature_093, feature_011, and feature_006.
feature_096, feature_064, feature_045, feature_042 (5-level ordinals) do NOT show significant chi-square association with objective_response.

analyses.code: Adjusted Logit added one binary at a time; ranked by p
analyses.result_summary: Most promising were feature_121 (coef -0.090, p=0.044) and feature_111 (coef -0.080, p=0.051). No binary reached p<0.01 independently. Treatment-like effects beyond feature_035 and feature_093 are weak at best.

analyses.code: chi-square on each multi-level ordinal vs response
analyses.result_summary: feature_096 p=0.18, feature_064 p=0.49, feature_045 p=0.09, feature_042 p=0.41 - none significant. Only feature_057 and (weakly) feature_125 among ordinals associate with response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effects of feature_035 and feature_093 on objective_response are approximately additive on the log-odds scale (no significant feature_035 x feature_093 interaction); the highest response rate occurs in patients with both feature_035=1 AND feature_093=1.

feature_093's positive effect attenuates in the highest-risk feature_057 stratum (interaction in the direction of waning benefit at higher feature_057 level), but the interaction is not statistically significant.

analyses.code: 2x2 cross-tab and Logit(y ~ f035 + f093 + f035:f093)
analyses.result_summary: Joint response rates: neither 16.4%, f093 only 17.9%, f035 only 21.8%, both 25.8%. Interaction coef +0.110 (p=0.452); not significant. Effects approximately additive.
Stratum-specific differences: f057=0 +1.8pp, f057=1 +2.6pp, f057=2 -0.7pp. Interaction with f057_2 coef -0.179 (p=0.235); descriptive attenuation but not statistically significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is no significant race x feature_035 interaction (i.e., the treatment effect of feature_035 does not differ across feature_005 categories).

There is no significant insurance x feature_035 interaction; treatment effect of feature_035 is similar across feature_087 categories (though point estimates suggest a slightly larger effect in medicare/medicaid than private).

analyses.code: Logit with f035, race dummies, and all 4 f035 x race dummies; LR test vs main-effects model
analyses.result_summary: LR statistic 2.06 on 4 df, p=0.726. Stratum-specific differences: white +6.5pp, asian +4.0pp, black +4.1pp, hispanic +4.1pp, other +5.4pp. No evidence of effect modification by race.

analyses.code: LR test of insurance x f035 interaction
analyses.result_summary: LR statistic 6.46 on 3 df, p=0.091; suggestive but not significant. Stratum-specific differences: medicaid +7.2pp, medicare +7.4pp, private +3.5pp, uninsured +5.8pp.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for feature_057, feature_011, and feature_006, exposure to feature_035 increases the marginal probability of objective_response by approximately 5 percentage points (from ~16.5% to ~21.9%).

After adjustment for feature_035, feature_011, and feature_006, moving from feature_057=0 to feature_057=2 lowers the marginal probability of objective_response by approximately 10 percentage points (from ~21.2% to ~11.2%).

feature_011 and feature_006 are independent of feature_057 (their distributions do not differ across feature_057 strata), supporting their interpretation as separate prognostic axes rather than surrogates for feature_057.

analyses.code: Marginal standardization: predict(X with f035=1) - predict(X with f035=0) averaged across sample, adjusted Logit model
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted marginal probabilities: f035=0 -> 0.1651, f035=1 -> 0.2194; average risk difference +0.0544.

analyses.code: Marginal standardization for feature_057 contrast 0 vs 2 in adjusted Logit model
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted marginal probabilities: f057=0 -> 0.2122, f057=2 -> 0.1119; average risk difference -0.1003.

analyses.code: groupby feature_057 mean of feature_011 and feature_006
analyses.result_summary: feature_011 mean by feature_057 level: 0->3.80, 1->3.90, 2->3.84; feature_006 by level: 59.85, 60.07, 59.83. Distributions effectively identical across strata; the prognostic impact of feature_011 and feature_006 is independent of feature_057.
